title: Enhancing Organizational Performance: Harnessing AI and NLP for User Feedback Analysis in Product Development

task: Text analysis involves the use of NLP techniques to process and analyze written customer feedback, such as reviews, support tickets, and surveys (Ravi & Ravi, 2015).
Rating analysis, on the other hand, involves analyzing numerical ratings that customers provide, such as star ratings or numerical scores (Lak & Turetken, 2014).
Both text analysis and rating analysis can be used together to provide a more comprehensive understanding of customer feedback.
Data Collection: This involves gathering user feedback from questionnaire and direct comments from users.
Data Cleaning: This involves removing any irrelevant or duplicate data and ensuring that the data is in a consistent format for analysis.
Data Analysis: This involves using various techniques to analyze the data, such as sentiment analysis, text classification, topic modeling, clustering, or regression analysis.
Results Interpretation: This involves interpreting the results of the data analysis to understand the key findings and insights and generate recommendations for action.
Feedback Implementation: This involves using the insights from the data analysis to make improvements to the product or service, enhance the customer experience, and drive business success.
population: The research used three crane products, respectively, XCMG XCR100U, Tadano GR1000XL and Grove GRT8100.
Number of valid comments for each product are XCMG: 76 Tadano: 49 Grove: 43
outcome_definitions: Sentiment Analysis: This is a method that uses natural language processing (NLP) and machine learning algorithms to classify user feedback.
Table 1 used mean and standard deviation to give the result of each product, and Figure. 1 listed positive (0.05+), negative (below -0.05), and neutral (-0.05-0.05) in sentiment.
A word cloud is a visual representation of the most frequently occurring words in a given text.
Topic Modeling: This is a method that uses NLP techniques to identify the underlying topics or themes present in a large corpus of user feedback.
statistical_results: Table. 1 Results of sentiment analysis of each product Brand Mean Std XCMG XCR100U -0.017 0.331 Tadano GR1000XL 0.050 0.234 Grove GRT8100 0.101 0.303
The attitude towards XCMG XCR100U is neural.
The attitude towards Tadano GR1000XL is between neural and positive.
The attitude towards Grove GRT8100 is positive.
Overall, Grove GRT8100 has the best reviews.
ambiguity_or_caveats: This type of study presents several limitations that should be acknowledged.
Firstly, the AI and NLP techniques applied are dependent on the quality and extent of the data available, which may affect the comprehensiveness of the user feedback analysis.
Limitations in the algorithms' ability to understand context and sarcasm could also lead to potential inaccuracies in sentiment analysis.
Additionally, the study focuses on a specific product type within a particular industry, which may limit the generalizability of the findings to other sectors or product categories.
There may also be inherent biases in the user feedback collected, as it may not represent the views of the entire customer base.

Future research could address these limitations by incorporating a broader data set, utilizing more advanced AI models capable of context recognition, and extending the analysis to multiple product types across various industries.
However, it's important to note that AI should be used as a tool to augment human analysis and decision-making, not replace it entirely.

notes: This is a case study / application paper demonstrating the use of AI and NLP techniques for analyzing user feedback on heavy machine crane products. It is not an experimental study comparing AI-assisted vs. non-AI-assisted conditions. The paper presents sentiment analysis results (means and standard deviations) for three products and qualitative findings from topic modeling and radar chart comparisons. No statistical tests or p-values are reported. The study has no control group or experimental manipulation.
